rulings.Case Title: WILLIAM DUTY,ET AL VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, September 4, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Defendant TRZ Realty, LLC¿s motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is denied as to issue 2 (premises owner/contractor liability) and off calendar as to issue 1 (products liability). Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that Mr. Duty was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Plaintiffs¿ responses to defendant¿s special interrogatories and Mr. Calhoun¿s deposition testimony preclude defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Plaintiffs represent that they have dismissed their products liability cause of action. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative is not contested, the prevailing party is required to bring a proposed order the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: RAYMOND GENE BENNETT et al VS. 3M COMPANY et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, September 4, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 3. Defendant Goulds Pumps, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant sustained its initial burden. Plaintiffs failed to present evidence creating a triable issue whether Mr. Bennett was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Defendant¿s objections to Mr. Powell¿s deposition testimony and defendant¿s interrogatory responses are overruled. Defendant¿s objections to plaintiffs¿ Exhibits B (GP Product News) and E (instructions) are sustained. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: LAVON HOELMER et al VS. 3M COMPANY et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, September 4, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 5. Defendant Groeniger & Co.¿s demurrer to the first amended complaint: Defendant¿s demurrer to the third cause of action for false representation is overruled. Plaintiffs¿ complaint contains sufficient allegations to state a cause of action for false representation (Restatement 402-B). Defendant¿s demurrer to the fourth cause of action for intentional tort/intentional failure to warn is sustained with 10 days leave to amend to give plaintiffs the opportunity, if they are able to do so, to state facts sufficient to constitute a cause of action pursuant to Civil Code sections 1708, 1709 and 1710. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)
